description: 30 - 14-09-2025 - Emails between Ailsa Terry (Private Secretary for Foreign Affairs, No.10) and
text:
others

Subject: RE: URGENT - Reply to FAC re. Mandelson CO/UKSV input
Date: Sunday, 14 September 2025 at 11:02:46 British Summer Time
From: Caroline Hurndall

To: Ailsa Terry, Jes , Symes, Elkie - CO (OFF-SEN)

CC: Serena Stone, lan Collard *, Dan York-Smith

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

Elkie, All,

To add onto the deadlines — the FS would like to see the annex (along with our draft
letter) by 1pm today. So I’m afraid we need to pull forward the deadline to send to us to
12.30pm please. Apologies — as per Ailsa’s email, we are seeking to get these all done
asap.

Best wishes

Caroline

Caroline Hurndall| PPS/FS | Personal

The Foreign Secretary's box deadline is midday, Monday to Thursday. Please allow a
minimum of 48hrs to process work sent to FSPO. Please also copy Jes to
all submissions/updates to the Foreign Secretary.

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

From: Ailsa Terry
Sent: 14 September 2025 10:57
To: JCS : Symes, Elkie - CO (OFF-SEN)
Cc: Serena Stone ; lan Collard * ; Caroline Hurndall ; Dan York-Smith
Subject: Subject: Re: URGENT - Reply to FAC re. Mandelson CO/UKSV input
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
JCS all

Many thanks all. To reiterate we want sight of this draft and relevant annexes asap
to release today.

Grateful for sight of the FCDO draft as close to 2pm today as possible. We know
this may not be final. If you need anything more from me to enable this to happen

please call.
Ailsa
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
From: Jcs Personal
Sent: Sunday, September 14, 2025 10:08:25 AM
To: Symes, Elkie - CO (OFF-SEN) Personal
Ce: Serena Stone Personal lan Collard *
Personal : Caroline Hurndall Personal
Ailsa Terry Personal

Subject: RE: URGENT - Reply to FAC re. Mandelson CO/UKSV input

83

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
--- Resending to correct email ----  
Elkie,  
Grateful if you can commission CO/UKSV to input into an Annex that will be shared 
with the FAC in response to their attached letter re. Mandelson. Grateful if we can have 
input by 2pm today please as we are aiming for FS to issue the letter today. Given 
FCDO do not own the security clearance process, the Foreign Secretary will not want 
to take responsibility for responding to the FAC on these matters. We therefore 
propose the FS write a short cover letter and an annex (owned by the Cabinet Office) 
with responses to their questions.  
To support your teams, the questions are set out below and I have included where we 
think the FCDO does have agency to answer the questions are they refer specifically 
to FCDO/the FS.  
Annex A - Cabinet Office response to the FAC questions  
1. What security concerns were raised by the agencies undertaking security
clearance ahead of Lord Mandelson’s appointment? [For CO to answer]
2. What was the response of the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development
Office to any such concerns? FCDO advice was not sought, nor given.
3. Was it the assessment of the FCDO that any such concerns represented a
potential barrier to Lord Mandelson’s appointment? FCDO advice was not
sought, nor given.
4. Were any conditions imposed on the remit of Lord Mandelson as Ambassador
in order to mitigate any security concerns? [For CO to answer]
5. Was there a decision made to dismiss security concerns? Was such a
decision taken by the FCDO or by No. 10? If so, by whom? [For CO to answer]
6. If no decision was taken to straightforwardly dismiss security concerns raised
by the relevant agencies, was a decision made to suspend or alter: (a) the
usual security vetting requirements; or, (b) the usual timeline for vetting
procedures? [For CO to answer]
7. Was it the assessment of the Foreign Secretary that there was sufficient time
between the announcement of Lord Mandelson’s appointment in December
and the beginning of his tenure on 10 February to carry out full thorough
vetting? Yes. While an expedited clearance process is faster than a routine
clearance, it is not unusual to do so, and within the bounds of the vetting
process.
8. Did the usual vetting process for diplomatic appointments commence in
relation to Lord Mandelson before his appointment was made public? [For CO
to answer]
Kind regards,  
 
 | Deputy Private Secretary to the Permanent Under 
Secretaries, Sir Oliver Robbins and Nick Dyer | Permanent Under-Secretaries’ Joint 
Private Office  
 | King Charles Street | Foreign, Commonwealth & Development 
Office Email:
 | Mob: 
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
JCS
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
JCS
84


Please ensure items for the PUSs’ daily e-box reach PUS Action by 1400 clearly marked for the relevant 
PS. Our portfolios can be found here.Please note that we will not routinely keep a record of any emails 
or attachments.  
Follow us online: www.gov.uk/fcdo 
This email is intended for the addressee(s) only: All messages sent and received by the Foreign, 
Commonwealth & Development Office may be monitored in line with relevant UK legislation  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
For latest news and information from Downing Street visit: http://www.gov.uk/number10 
Follow Number 10 on X (Twitter): http://x.com/10DowningStreet  
Help save paper - do you need to print this email?  
This email and any files transmitted with it are intended solely for the use of the individual(s) to whom 
they are addressed. If you are not the intended recipient and have received this email in error, please 
notify the sender and delete the email.  
This footnote also confirms that our email communications may be monitored to ensure the secure and 
effective operation of our systems and for other lawful purposes, and that this email has been swept 
for malware and viruses.  
Follow us online: www.gov.uk/fcdo 
This email is intended for the addressee(s) only: All messages sent and received by the 
Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office may be monitored in line with relevant UK 
legislation

Redacted addresses appear as 'PERSONAL' — names are retained. Build sender→recipient edges per message for communication graph.
